text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a central repository. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a build. This automated build compiles the code and runs a series of tests to check for errors. By running tests on every change, developers can catch issues early, often within minutes, instead of finding problems much later. This makes bugs easier and cheaper to fix. CI pipelines also ensure that the codebase stays stable and ready for further development.
